issues_found: Future publication date (March 2026) referencing 2025 data is unusual but source authenticity appears valid; New York Fed Liberty Street Economics is a credible source
Event type 'cost_dynamics_report' is appropriate for this content

Topic classification under 'market structure' is somewhat tangential; this is primarily a regional economic conditions/macro report with secondary market structure relevance through sector cost differentials
human_review_reason: Source is a credible Federal Reserve Bank publication with strong factual support for all major claims. All numerical values in key_points are directly traceable to source data. Regional scope limitations are properly acknowledged. The unusual publication date referencing past-year data appears to be standard Fed blog practice. No unsupported claims identified.

corrected_fields.market_structure_context: Regional business survey data from the New York-Northern New Jersey area shows structural differences in cost composition between manufacturers and service firms, with manufacturers facing larger utilities and materials cost burdens due to greater tariff exposure. The survey uses trimmed mean methodology excluding the highest and lowest 5% of responses, which provides robust central tendency estimates but may understate extreme experiences.
